summary: Feature request to expose the private canvasSize field as a public CanvasSize property on SKCanvasView and SKGLView in SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor, matching the pattern already used by WPF, WinUI, and Uno platform views.

repoLinks.description: PR #1832 (merged) — exposed Dpi property on Blazor views, same pattern requested here
PR #1912 (closed without merge) — community PR that added public SKSize CanvasSize => canvasSize to both Blazor views
Discussion #1888 — community discussion suggesting CanvasSize exposure

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: canvasSize is still a private field in both SKCanvasView.razor.cs and SKGLView.razor.cs on the main branch. PR #1912 was closed without merge.

analysis.summary: The Blazor SKCanvasView and SKGLView components both have a private canvasSize field that tracks the HTML canvas element size, but unlike WPF (SKElement.CanvasSize), WinUI (SKXamlCanvas.CanvasSize), and Uno (SKXamlCanvas.CanvasSize) views, this field is not exposed as a public property. PR #1832 already merged the same pattern for the Dpi property. A community PR #1912 was submitted to fix this but was closed without merge or reviewer feedback.
analysis.rationale: Classified as type/enhancement rather than type/bug because CanvasSize is a convention across views rather than a contractual interface requirement, and the Blazor component functions correctly — it just lacks a convenience accessor. The fix is trivial: add public SKSize CanvasSize => canvasSize; to both components. Confidence is high because the request is well-specified and the pattern is already established.

keySignals.text: analogous to #1831 & #1832 it may is useful to expose the canvasSize Property, too
keySignals.interpretation: Reporter correctly identifies the Dpi-exposure pattern from PR #1832 and requests the same for canvasSize.

keySignals.text: Add a public property on both components to expose the private field
keySignals.interpretation: Clear, well-scoped request — the reporter understands the implementation is trivial.

keySignals.text: anyone who wants to do non-trivial JS interop needs the htmlCanvas ElementReference exposed
keySignals.interpretation: Related enhancement request — the commenter wants broader access to Blazor component internals for JS interop.

codeInvestigation.finding: canvasSize is declared as private SKSize — not publicly accessible. Dpi was exposed (line 65) as public double Dpi => dpi; by PR #1832, but the same was never done for canvasSize.
Same pattern — canvasSize is private SKSize with no public accessor. Dpi is exposed (line 72).
WPF view exposes public SKSize CanvasSize { get; private set; } — the property Blazor is missing.
WinUI view also exposes public SKSize CanvasSize { get; private set; } — confirms cross-platform convention.

workarounds: Inside OnPaintSurface handler, use e.Info.Size to get the canvas dimensions (pixel size). This only works within the paint callback, not for external layout logic.
nextQuestions: Should htmlCanvas ElementReference also be exposed (as requested by commenter simonsarris)?
Why was PR #1912 closed without merge or feedback?

resolution.hypothesis: The canvasSize field was kept private as an oversight when the Blazor views were first implemented, not as a deliberate design choice. All other platform views expose it publicly.

proposals.title: Add public CanvasSize property to both Blazor views
proposals.description: Add public SKSize CanvasSize => canvasSize; to both SKCanvasView.razor.cs and SKGLView.razor.cs, matching the Dpi pattern already in place.

proposals.title: Use e.Info.Size inside paint handler
proposals.description: As a workaround, access the canvas size via SKPaintSurfaceEventArgs.Info.Size within the OnPaintSurface callback. This provides the pixel dimensions but is only available during paint.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Add public CanvasSize property to both Blazor views
resolution.recommendedReason: Trivial one-liner that matches established patterns (Dpi property, other platform views). PR #1912 already demonstrated the exact implementation.

actionability.reason: Well-specified enhancement with a trivial implementation. PR #1912 demonstrates the fix but was closed without explanation — needs maintainer decision on whether to reopen or submit a new PR.

actions.description: Apply enhancement, Blazor views, and WASM labels
Acknowledge the request, note the parity gap and the workaround
actions.comment:
Thanks for the well-scoped request. You're right that `canvasSize` should be exposed — every other platform view (WPF, WinUI, Uno) has a public `CanvasSize` property, and the `Dpi` property was already exposed on the Blazor views via PR #1832.

As a workaround until this is addressed, you can access the canvas dimensions inside your paint handler via `e.Info.Size`:

```csharp
void OnPaintSurface(SKPaintSurfaceEventArgs e)
{
    var size = e.Info.Size; // pixel dimensions
}
```

This won't help if you need the size outside the paint callback, but it covers most use cases.

actions.description: Link to the closed PR #1912 that attempted this fix
